# Archero LSPosed config generated by Codex always-on gameplay profile
#
# Parser rules:
# - The native module ignores lines that start with #.
# - Keep comments on their own lines. Do not write inline comments after values.
# - Good: gold_add_scale=1
# - Bad:  gold_add_scale=1 # comment, because bool parsing expects only 0 or 1.
# - The only authoritative config path is:
#   /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/com.habby.archero/files/archero_mod_config.txt
# - The native module reloads this file about every 2 seconds while Archero is running.
#
# Value format:
# - Boolean options use 0 for OFF and 1 for ON.
# - Numeric options are clamped by the native module.
# - Manual edits work as long as the key=value line remains exact.

# headshot
# Forces EntityData.GetHeadShot behavior on the native side.
# 1 enables the startup hook behavior. 0 lets the original game result pass through.
# Example: headshot=1
headshot=1

# godmode
# Forces EntityData.GetMiss behavior for the hero on the native side.
# 1 enables the startup hook behavior. 0 lets the original game result pass through.
# Example: godmode=1
godmode=1

# damage_v1
# Forces TableTool.PlayerCharacter_UpgradeModel.GetATKBase to the module's high damage value.
# This replaces the old external Damage [v1] startup patch.
damage_v1=1

# health
# Forces TableTool.PlayerCharacter_UpgradeModel.GetHPMaxBase to the module's high HP value.
# This replaces the old external Health startup patch.
health=1

# attack_speed
# Forces Weapon_weapon.get_Speed and get_AttackSpeed to the module's fast attack value.
# Current native value is 100.0.
# This replaces the old external Attack Speed startup patch.
attack_speed=1

# shoot_through_walls
# Forces Weapon_weapon.get_bThroughWall true.
# This replaces the old external Shoot --Walls--> startup patch.
shoot_through_walls=1

# walk_through_water
# Injects the real SkillAlone2080 water-walk ability for the hero at battle init when available.
# Also mirrors EntityBase.bFlyWater, EntityData.mFlyWaterCount, and the native SetFlyWater collider path.
# Water still spawns; this does not touch MapCreator water generation.
walk_through_water=1

# walk_through_walls
# Mirrors EntityBase.bFlyStone, EntityData.mFlyStoneCount, SetFlyStone/SetFlyAll collider state,
# and the hero check_pos traversal bypass.
# Walls, obstacles, shops, angels, and water still spawn; this does not touch MapCreator generation.
# It defaults ON with the always-on gameplay profile.
walk_through_walls=1

# inject_greed_skill
# Injects the battle skill ID 1000040 at hero battle init through metadata-resolved EntityBase.AddSkill.
# This is the runtime-confirmed Greed slot ID adjacent to Smart and backed by the game's Skill_greedyskill table.
# The status file confirms whether EntityBase.ContainsSkill accepts it in the running game.
# It may not appear in the acquired-skills UI because Greed uses a separate greedyskill table/display path.
inject_greed_skill=1

# inject_smart_skill
# Injects the named Smart slot skill ID 1000041 at hero battle init through metadata-resolved EntityBase.AddSkill.
# The status file confirms whether EntityBase.ContainsSkill accepts it in the running game.
# Smart should normally appear in the acquired-skills UI.
inject_smart_skill=1

# game_speed
# Forces UnityEngine.Time.timeScale from the native module.
# This replaces the old GameGuardian speedhack path, so the game cannot reset it back to 1 through Time.timeScale writes.
# 1 keeps the speed multiplier applied at startup and refreshed while the game runs. 0 lets game timeScale pass through normally.
game_speed=1

# game_speed_multiplier
# The forced UnityEngine.Time.timeScale value when game_speed=1.
# Range in native: 0.1 to 20. Default is 4.
# Example normal speed: game_speed=0 or game_speed_multiplier=1
# Example default: game_speed_multiplier=4
game_speed_multiplier=4

# install_gold_hooks
# Master switch for optional gold/material/drop hooks.
# 1 installs the optional hook families requested below. 0 leaves only startup gameplay hooks.
# Keep this OFF unless the owned server-side settlement validator accepts the same reward rules.
# Once hooks are installed in a running process they are not physically uninstalled until restart,
# but the per-feature flags below still gate most hook behavior.
# Example safe/default: install_gold_hooks=0
install_gold_hooks=0

# force_server_validation
# Installs pass-through hooks on the client's post-battle settlement-validation
# entry points so an owned-server operator can confirm each path actually runs:
#   - BattleModuleData.BuildCheatData()
#   - HTTPSendClient.CheckGameOverCheat(NetCacheOne)
#   - LocalSave.BattleIn_DropEquipByServer
#   - LocalSave.BattleIn_DropEquipDataByTransId(uint)
#   - GameOverModeCtrlBase.CheckDropEquipsByServer(...)
# Each hook calls the original implementation and bumps a counter in
# archero_mod_status.txt under hits.validate_*. The hooks never modify return
# values or fabricate server responses, so they cannot bypass server-side
# validation; they exist only to make the validation flow observable so a
# server operator can verify their backend receives a settlement check for
# every battle. Resolution is metadata-only via class+method+arg_count.
# Example safe/default: force_server_validation=0
force_server_validation=0

# tiny_direct_patch
# Directly patches tiny 8-byte IL2CPP stubs that are too small for normal inline hooks.
# This is riskier than normal hooks. It is OFF in the balanced profile.
# Full 32-bit gold caps are not forced through tiny coin-cap stubs; concrete int32 hooks handle that.
# Example safe/default: tiny_direct_patch=0
tiny_direct_patch=0

# gold_add_scale
# Scales BattleModuleData.AddGold(float/int) by gold_multiplier.
# This is the main balanced coin boost path because it changes the amount when gold is added.
# Example: with gold_multiplier=2, a 100 coin add becomes about 200.
# Example safe/default: gold_add_scale=0
gold_add_scale=0

# gold_get_fixed
# Forces stage gold read methods to stage_gold_fixed instead of their original value.
# This can create mismatches if other stage state still expects the original value.
# Use only for testing a fixed stage total. Safer default is 0.
# Example risky test: gold_get_fixed=1 with stage_gold_fixed=5000
gold_get_fixed=0

# gold_get_scale
# Scales stage gold read methods by gold_multiplier.
# Less blunt than gold_get_fixed, but still touches read-side totals and may duplicate other scaling.
# Safer default is 0 when gold_add_scale is already enabled.
# Example test: gold_get_scale=1 with gold_multiplier=2
gold_get_scale=0

# gold_update_fixed
# Forces LocalSave.BattleIn_UpdateGold writes to stage_gold_fixed.
# This is a high-impact write-side hook and can cause consistency checks to notice impossible totals.
# Safer default is 0.
# Example risky test: gold_update_fixed=1 with stage_gold_fixed=10000
gold_update_fixed=0

# gold_update_scale
# Scales LocalSave.BattleIn_UpdateGold writes by gold_multiplier.
# This changes the in-stage saved gold update path and can stack with other gold scaling.
# Safer default is 0.
# Example test: gold_update_scale=1 with gold_multiplier=2
gold_update_scale=0

# gold_formula_scale
# Scales EntityData.getGold formula output by gold_multiplier.
# This affects gold calculated from entity/drop formulas before later add/save paths may see it.
# Can stack with other paths, so leave OFF unless isolating this formula path.
# Example test: gold_formula_scale=1
gold_formula_scale=0

# gold_static_scale
# Scales static GameConfig coin/box gold values by gold_multiplier.
# This targets config-derived values such as wave, box-drop, and box-choice gold candidates.
# Useful for experiments, but balanced profile keeps it OFF to avoid stacked multipliers.
# Example test: gold_static_scale=1
gold_static_scale=0

# gold_drop_scalar
# Scales scalar drop methods by gold_multiplier.
# These are lower-level drop amount paths and can overlap with list/add/update hooks.
# Safer default is 0.
# Example test: gold_drop_scalar=1
gold_drop_scalar=0

# gold_drop_repeat
# Repeats selected gold drop mutator calls gold_drop_repeats times.
# This is much more aggressive than simple scaling and is more likely to create validation issues.
# Safer default is 0.
# Example risky test: gold_drop_repeat=1 with gold_drop_repeats=2
gold_drop_repeat=0

# gold_ratio_scale
# Scales mode/stage gold ratios and related GetDropDataGold style paths.
# This can affect multiple game modes and may stack with add/list/drop scaling.
# Safer default is 0.
# Example test: gold_ratio_scale=1 with gold_multiplier=2
gold_ratio_scale=0

# gold_list_scale
# Scales gold entries inside BattleDropData lists by gold_multiplier.
# This edits list item data after the list exists. It can overlap with scalar and add hooks.
# Safer default is 0.
# Example test: gold_list_scale=1
gold_list_scale=0

# gold_save_realtime
# Forces GameLogic.CanSaveGoldInRealTime to true.
# This is intended to make realtime in-stage gold saving possible when that path is checked.
# It does not itself create gold. Safer default is 0.
# Example test: gold_save_realtime=1
gold_save_realtime=0

# material_drop_repeat
# Repeats selected material/equipment drop mutator calls material_drop_repeats times.
# This covers many non-gold drops such as equipment, stones, scrolls, cookies, soul stones,
# and some coin-like special drops that are routed through material-style drop functions.
# Example safe/default: material_drop_repeat=0
material_drop_repeat=0

# max_drop_cap_patch
# Enables max-drop cap hooks and the chapter/session loot-total bypass.
# Normal chapters use concrete StageLevelManager caps; those are raised here too.
# DropManager's per-chapter total counters are cleared while this is ON so repeated drops do not stop at the old run-wide limit.
# DropManager.CanGetMoreDrop/getTotalCnt/AppendDropCount and LocalSave equip-drop gates are also relaxed.
# Item/material caps use max_drop_cap_value.
# Gold/coin caps use max_gold_cap_value through concrete int32 hooks.
# This is meant to keep local cap reads high enough for boosted drops.
# Example safe/default: max_drop_cap_patch=0
max_drop_cap_patch=0

# gold_multiplier
# Multiplier used by scale-style gold hooks and some list-scaling paths.
# Native range: 0 to 100000, but high values can trip consistency checks.
# Balanced example: 2. Aggressive test example: 16.
# Example safe/default: gold_multiplier=2
gold_multiplier=2

# stage_gold_fixed
# Fixed amount used only when a fixed-value hook is enabled, such as gold_get_fixed or gold_update_fixed.
# 0 is fine when fixed hooks are OFF. This value is not the max gold cap.
# Example fixed test: stage_gold_fixed=5000
# Example safe/default: stage_gold_fixed=0
stage_gold_fixed=0

# gold_drop_repeats
# Number of times selected gold drop mutators run when gold_drop_repeat=1.
# Range: 1 to 50. The loop count includes the original call replacement count.
# Keep low; repeating gold drops is riskier than gold_add_scale.
# Example safe/default: gold_drop_repeats=1
gold_drop_repeats=1

# material_drop_repeats
# Number of times selected material/equipment drop mutators run when material_drop_repeat=1.
# Range: 1 to 50. The loop count includes the original call replacement count.
# Balanced example: 2. Higher values can produce suspicious item counts.
# Example safe/default: material_drop_repeats=2
material_drop_repeats=2

# max_drop_cap_value
# Item/material max-drop cap returned by material cap hooks.
# Range in this build: 1 to 65535. This is intentionally not 2 billion because these are item counts.
# It affects item/material caps, not the main gold/coin cap.
# Example safe/default: max_drop_cap_value=65535
max_drop_cap_value=65535

# max_gold_cap_value
# Gold/coin max-drop cap returned by concrete int32 gold cap hooks.
# Range: 1 to 2147483647. This is separate from max_drop_cap_value.
# 2000000000 is below signed int32 max and matches the high coin cap target.
# Example high/default: max_gold_cap_value=2000000000
max_gold_cap_value=2000000000
